stance: Risk-on within small-caps: a breadth-heavy, analyst-revision-tilted momentum book; prune the falling-revision tail.
experience_note: No prior round to grade (last_round_review is null), so this is a pre-registered baseline. What I am watching: (1) whether the revision tilt actually pays — if my high-ARM new buys (UFBL, HONASA, GUJGASLTD, EQUITASBNK, POONAWALLA, etc.) as a group beat the falling-revision tail I exited (CEATLTD, KEC, WHIRLPOOL, ABREL, EMAMILTD, RHIM) over the next 1-3 months, the analyst-revision thesis is working; if they don't, revision momentum is not transferring in this regime and I should de-emphasise it next round. (2) Whether breadth helped or hurt — if a few mid-weight names drove all the active return, I oversized diversification. (3) Conviction-IC: did I size eventual winners bigger? I keep my highest-revision and best-momentum names at the top of the weight ladder so this is testable. I would change my mind if price momentum re-disperses (z_mom spread widens) — then I would lean back on momentum rather than leaning so heavily on revisions. I am explicitly NOT re-chasing the thin high-z_mom oddities even if they pop, because I pre-registered them as artifacts.
guardrail_notes: dropped 17 non-priceable/unknown ticker(s): MEESHO, POLICYBZR, ATHERENERG, ANGELONE, MCX, POWERINDIA, APARINDS, TDPOWERSYS
